title: Birthday Sushi Overload
summary: One of Ji (my sister) and Hunter's (my brother-in-law) wedding gifts was an electric hotpot. For the uninitiated, hotpot is a Korean dish where you have a pot of simmering broth that you dunk vegetables, noodles and meat in to cook. For dinner our first night, we went to the Asian grocery store K-Mart and stocked up on supplies for a dinner of hot pot. We had a lovely dinner, dunking thin slices of beef, fish balls, mushrooms and cabbage into a chili and dashi broth. The following day was rainy and gloomy, so we played some indoor pickleball before braving the rain and visiting the farmer's market for lunch. For dinner, we took Ji and Hunter to a restaurant called Le Pigeon for their wedding gift. Le Pigeon is a French restaurant with a tasting menu and is well renowned for its unique ingredients and flavor combinations. I first heard about Le Pigeon on the Travel Network show Bizarre Foods when I was in high school. I remember thinking it looked so interesting and I have wanted to visit ever since. The dinner did not disappoint. We left feeling very full and satisfied after eating five courses including a rabbit pie and foie gras ice cream. The following day was Ji's birthday, which we celebrated by going for a hike in the Colombia River Gorge and soaking at a hot springs on the way home. For dinner, we went to an all-you-can-eat sushi restaurant with Ji and Hunter's friends. The sushi was delicious, but Helen and I were both battling tummy aches on the drive home from our overindulgence. Our last day in Vancouver was full of chores. My big chore for the day was to de-ice the refrigerator in the van. What I thought would be a quick and easy task quickly became an epic, as what felt like gallons of water came pouring out of the fridge onto the van floor. As an added bonus, I spilled a bottle of fish sauce in the clean up process, which left our van with a lovely stinky feet aroma. For dinner, we made taco bowls and Ji's friends, Liv and Doug, came over to join us.

All of us hiking at the Lyle Cherry Orchard.
Just a small fraction of the sushi we ate that night.
